Title: Cold stress regulates accumulation of flavonoids and terpenoids in plants by phytohormone, transcription process, functional enzyme, and epigenetics

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced ACS expression is associated with increased ethylene biosynthesis that promotes flavonoid, especially anthocyanin, accumulation in plum.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated after cold stress (0 or 5°C) followed by transfer to 25°C in postharvest fruit.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after cold stress (0 or 5°C) followed by transfer to 25°C in postharvest fruit.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0 or 5°C cold stress followed by shift to 25°C; postharvest condition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression analysis cited in review; metabolite profiling in postharvest fruit
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cold stress increased ACS transcript levels in plum fruit. 2. Increased ACS likely elevated ethylene production. 3. Ethylene was proposed to mediate higher anthocyanin and total flavonoid accumulation after rewarming.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential leverage point for postharvest induction of flavonoids through ethylene-related regulation under cold treatment.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review summarizes ACS as an upstream hormone-biosynthesis gene rather than a direct flavonoid-pathway enzyme.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced ACO expression is associated with enhanced ethylene production and subsequent anthocyanin/flavonoid accumulation in plum.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated after cold stress followed by transfer to 25°C.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated after cold stress followed by transfer to 25°C.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 0 or 5°C cold stress followed by shift to 25°C; postharvest condition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression analysis cited in review; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACO expression increased under cold treatment in plum. 2. Ethylene biosynthesis was enhanced. 3. Elevated ethylene was linked to increased anthocyanin accumulation after cold storage.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for postharvest metabolic regulation strategies exploiting cold-triggered ethylene biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included by the review as a hormone-biosynthesis component affecting flavonoid accumulation indirectly.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdMIEL1 negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation by ubiquitinating MdMYB308L and MdMYB1; cold reduces this negative effect via MdMIEL1 protein degradation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast screening assay; protein ubiquitination/degradation evidence; cold treatment response summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMIEL1 encodes a RING E3 ligase that ubiquitinates MdMYB308L and MdMYB1. 2. This causes degradation of positive anthocyanin regulators. 3. Cold accelerates MdMIEL1 degradation, relieving repression of anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for gene editing to enhance cold-induced apple coloration.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broader effects on cold tolerance and protein turnover networks.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a post-translational regulator rather than a TF or biosynthetic enzyme.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdBT2 negatively affects anthocyanin accumulation by promoting MdMYB23 degradation; cold inhibits MdBT2 transcription and stabilizes MdMYB23.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cold-response transcription analysis and protein stability relationship summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdBT2 promotes degradation of MdMYB23 protein. 2. Cold suppresses MdBT2 transcription. 3. Stabilized MdMYB23 under cold promotes anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to increase anthocyanin accumulation by reducing turnover of positive MYB regulators.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May affect other ubiquitin-dependent signaling processes.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a TF or pathway enzyme; treated here as a common regulatory component.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdSIZ1 promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis under cool temperature by sumoylating MdMYB1 and protecting it from ubiquitination.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and sumoylation/stability evidence summarized in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 17°C increases MdSIZ1 expression. 2. MdSIZ1 sumoylates MdMYB1. 3. Sumoylation inhibits MdMYB1 ubiquitination, increasing its stability and anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to enhance stable red coloration in apple under cool environments.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Post-translational modifier; classified as Common Gene.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Pho1a participates in starch degradation during cold storage of potato tubers, increasing monosaccharides that can support cold adaptation and glycosylation processes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with dormant tubers during long-term 4°C storage.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with dormant tubers during long-term 4°C storage.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Physiological and biochemical characterization cited in review
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cold-stored potato tubers degrade starch during dormancy. 2. Pho1a participates in this degradation process. 3. Resulting monosaccharides may serve as cryoprotectants and glycosyl donor pools.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant to cold storage metabolism and precursor supply for glycosylation-related quality traits.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review discusses Pho1a as supporting sugar-ligand supply rather than directly controlling flavonoid/terpenoid synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step to flavonoid and anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, initiating phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; committed entry to phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: first key enzyme gene in phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold stress generally increases PAL expression and activity, contributing to higher flavonoid and anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Multiple plant species including Ginkgo biloba, Prunus salicina and others summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under low temperature in several species.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Typically 4–15°C or cool night conditions depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaves; fruits; skins depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/expression analysis; enzyme activity assays; metabolite profiling across cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL is repeatedly induced by cold in flavonoid-accumulating tissues. 2. PAL activity often rises together with flavonoid content. 3. It is a major upstream contributor to cold-enhanced phenylpropanoid flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream engineering node for boosting broad flavonoid accumulation under cold-responsive production systems.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoids/anthocyanins contribute to stress defense and have human health benefits including antioxidant and cardiovascular protection.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates trans-cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold increases C4H expression/activity in several systems, supporting higher flavonoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatments around 4–15°C depending on study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; enzyme activity assays; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H is repeatedly reported as cold-responsive. 2. Its activity correlates with increased flavonoid content. 3. It supports enhanced flux into downstream flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with PAL/4CL or downstream branch genes to reinforce cold-driven flavonoid production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamate to its CoA thioester for entry into downstream phenylpropanoid/flavonoid synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold enhances 4CL expression/activity, contributing to increased flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatments in leaves or fruit tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; enzyme activity assays; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL is listed among cold-responsive flavonoid biosynthetic genes. 2. Cold often elevates its activity together with PAL and C4H. 3. This supports increased synthesis of downstream flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream engineering target for strengthening flux into flavonoid branches.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone, the entry scaffold of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold upregulates CHS transcription in grape skin and other systems, contributing to anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased in berry skin under low night temperature (10–11°C).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Molecular expression analysis and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS is induced by low temperature in grape skin. 2. This accompanies faster berry anthocyanin accumulation. 3. CHS participates in cold-enhanced activation of the anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful with MYB/UFGT engineering to improve cool-season berry coloration.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones to form dihydroflavonols used for anthocyanin/flavonol synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold induces F3H transcription in grape skin, supporting enhanced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and anthocyanin measurement
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. F3H transcription rises under cool nights in grape. 2. Increased expression accompanies accelerated anthocyanin accumulation. 3. F3H supports midstream flux toward pigmented flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target in cool-climate fruit coloration improvement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the late anthocyanin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important late biosynthetic gene frequently targeted by cold-responsive TFs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive TFs frequently activate DFR, leading to increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple species including apple and purple kale
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding assays (EMSA); transactivation evidence in cited studies; expression/metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR is a major downstream target of cold-induced MYB/bHLH TFs. 2. Its promoter contains low-temperature-responsive or TF-binding elements. 3. Elevated DFR expression is consistently associated with anthocyanin accumulation under cold.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong late-pathway target for combination with cold-responsive transcriptional regulators.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate anthocyanidin products before glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ANS is activated by several cold-responsive MYB/bHLH regulators and contributes to enhanced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR/transactivation evidence summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS is among the principal anthocyanin genes activated under cold. 2. It is a target of BoPAP1 and MdBBX20-related regulation in cited work. 3. Higher ANS expression supports elevated anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suitable late-pathway target to combine with upstream TFs for stronger pigmentation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to anthocyanidins to produce stable anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: last step and particularly important for anthocyanin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold elevates UFGT expression and/or activity, strongly promoting anthocyanin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Multiple species including grape and apple
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low night temperatures; 17°C or 4°C depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: fruit skin/peel and other pigmented tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; enzyme activity assays; promoter binding/transactivation by TFs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UFGT is a decisive late anthocyanin gene enhanced under cold. 2. Its promoter is directly activated by MdbHLH3 and influenced by MdMYBPA1-related regulation. 3. UFGT activity closely correlates with flavonoid glycoside accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent terminal-step target for improving stable anthocyanin accumulation and coloration.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Adds UDP-rhamnose to cyanidin or cyanidin 3-O-glucoside to form rhamnosylated anthocyanins.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold strongly induces UGT79B2/B3, promoting synthesis of glycosylated anthocyanins in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme function and cold induction summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT79B2/B3 catalyze cold-induced anthocyanin rhamnosylation. 2. These enzymes increase formation of more complex anthocyanin glycosides. 3. The review highlights glycosyltransferases as important cold-responsive metabolism genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important candidates for mining cold-inducible UGTs to diversify metabolite glycosides.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts DXP to MEP in the plastidial isoprenoid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme gene reported in taxol and blinin/carotenoid-related responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold induces DXR expression in taxol-producing systems and other terpenoid contexts, supporting increased terpenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Taxus; Conyza blinii and others summarized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and metabolite accumulation in cited studies
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DXR is repeatedly listed among cold-responsive terpenoid biosynthetic genes. 2. Induction of DXR accompanies enhanced taxol or other terpenoid accumulation. 3. It serves as a major upstream precursor-supply enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream target for boosting plastidial terpenoid precursor flux under cold induction.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces HMG-CoA to mevalonate in the MVA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold and ABA-associated signaling increase HMGR expression, contributing to elevated terpenoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress; ABA treatment; 4°C + MJ in some studies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and metabolite profiling summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HMGR is highlighted as a key cold-responsive enzyme gene in terpenoid production. 2. Its expression rises in ABA-treated A. annua and cold-stimulated Taxus systems. 3. Increased HMGR supports higher precursor flux to valuable terpenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for stacking with downstream pathway genes or regulators in terpenoid engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP and DMAPP units to form FPP, precursor for sesquiterpenes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold or ABA-related induction of FPS supports increased sesquiterpenoid production, including artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold stress; ABA treatment; cold storage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression and metabolite analyses summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FPS transcript accumulation is promoted under cold-associated conditions. 2. This supports enhanced sesquiterpene precursor availability. 3. FPS is one of the pathway genes elevated during artemisinin induction.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for improving sesquiterpenoid precursor supply in medicinal plants.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes FPP to amorpha-4,11-diene, the committed sesquiterpene precursor for artemisinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in artemisinin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold strongly increases ADS transcript levels, promoting artemisinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced up to about 10-fold at 4°C in cited study.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 4°C cold stress; cooler field environments around 15°C
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter-binding/transactivation via several TFs; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADS is one of the most strongly cold-induced artemisinin genes. 2. Multiple cold/JA-responsive TFs bind or activate its promoter. 3. Elevated ADS is tightly linked to higher artemisinin content.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime engineering target for increasing artemisinin yield, especially with TF stacking.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: oxidized artemisinin pathway intermediates including artemisinic alcohol/aldehyde/acid
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes sequential oxidations of amorpha-4,11-diene derivatives in the artemisinin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold and hormone signaling increase CYP71AV1 expression, contributing to greater artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased up to about 7-fold under 4°C in cited work.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; promoter activation by AaERF1/2; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP71AV1 is a major cold-responsive artemisinin-pathway gene. 2. It is targeted by JA/ERF-related regulators. 3. Higher CYP71AV1 expression accompanies increased artemisinin synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential co-target with ADS and DBR2/ALDH1 for enhanced artemisinin engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces artemisinic aldehyde toward the dihydroartemisinic acid branch leading to artemisinin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DBR2 is positively regulated by cold-responsive TFs and contributes to enhanced artemisinin accumulation under cold.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activation by AaORA/AaTCP14 and cold-associated expression analyses summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBR2 encodes an indispensable enzyme in the productive artemisinin branch. 2. Cold-responsive TFs synergistically activate its promoter. 3. This helps shift flux toward artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable branch-control target to improve artemisinin:precursor ratios.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes aldehyde intermediate to dihydroartemisinic acid in the artemisinin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-associated regulatory networks activate ALDH1, promoting artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activation by AaORA/AaTCP14 and direct binding by AaABF3 summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALDH1 is a key late gene in artemisinin biosynthesis. 2. It is directly activated by the ABA-responsive TF AaABF3 and by AaORA/AaTCP14 module. 3. This contributes to cold-enhanced artemisinin production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for stacking with ABA/JA-responsive regulators.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Forms DXP from pyruvate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate, initiating the MEP pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AaDXS is significantly upregulated early during 4°C treatment, indicating enhanced precursor supply for artemisinin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated at 6 h and 12 h under 4°C.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaDXS responds rapidly to cold. 2. It likely increases MEP-pathway precursor availability. 3. This supports downstream artemisinin accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Upstream precursor-supply gene for artemisinin engineering under inducible systems.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes GGPP, universal precursor for diterpenoids and carotenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme gene in taxol-related response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold induces GGPP synthase expression, supporting increased taxol and related diterpenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and metabolite accumulation summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GGPP synthase is cold responsive in diterpenoid-producing plants. 2. Its induction correlates with higher taxol or diterpenoid levels. 3. Promoter LTRE motifs may contribute to cold responsiveness in some species.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong precursor-pathway target for diterpenoid metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates 10-deacetylbaccatin III to baccatin III in the taxol pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme gene in taxol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold, especially with MJ, elevates DBAT expression and supports increased taxol production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and taxol quantification summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DBAT is one of the principal cold-responsive taxol genes. 2. Combined cold and MJ treatment enhances its expression and taxol accumulation. 3. It is part of the sustained cold-induced taxol biosynthetic response.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key late-pathway candidate for taxol production systems.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes oxidosqualene to dammarenediol-II, precursor of dammarane-type ginsenosides.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point in ginsenoside pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Intermittent cold stimulates DDS-associated pathway activity and increases PPD-type ginsenoside accumulation in ginseng cell culture.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression association and metabolite profiling summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DDS is listed among cold-responsive genes in ginsenoside accumulation studies. 2. Intermittent cold favors PPD-type ginsenoside production. 3. DDS functions at a key committed step of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for controlled-environment ginsenoside production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation steps leading to protopanaxadiol formation in ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive PPDS expression is associated with enhanced PPD-type ginsenoside accumulation in ginseng cultures.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression association and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPDS responds during cold-induced ginsenoside accumulation. 2. Intermittent cold favors the PPD branch. 3. PPDS helps determine ginsenoside composition under temperature regimes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning PPD/PPT ratios in ginseng production.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes hydroxylation steps converting PPD-related intermediates toward PPT formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Continuous cold treatment favors PPT-type ginsenoside accumulation, implicating PPTS-responsive branch control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression association and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PPTS is associated with cold-dependent shifts in ginsenoside composition. 2. Continuous cold promotes the PPT branch compared with intermittent cold. 3. Temperature therefore affects branch allocation in ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for selective production of PPT-type ginsenosides under defined cultivation regimes.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cytochrome P450; high homology to CYP716A12
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed oxidation enzyme directly or indirectly involved in ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PgCYP736B is strongly induced by cold and MeJA and is considered involved in ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Increased about 8.3-fold at 72 h after cold stimulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and homology inference summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgCYP736B is a cold-responsive P450 in ginseng. 2. It is also MeJA inducible. 3. The gene is inferred to participate directly or indirectly in ginsenoside biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact catalytic assignment remains uncertain in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for further validation and pathway engineering in ginseng.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes conversion of PPD to PPT, affecting ginsenoside branch composition.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP716A53v2 expression is negatively correlated with average temperature, implicating cooler conditions in promoting PPT-branch formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis summarized in review
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP716A53v2 is a key enzyme converting PPD to PPT. 2. Its expression is negatively correlated with temperature. 3. Temperature likely influences ginsenoside composition through this branch enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to manipulate PPD/PPT ratio for desired pharmacological profiles.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes artemisinin biosynthesis under cold-responsive JA-related regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of key artemisinin pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding assays summarized in review; Y1H/dual-luciferase through upstream AabHLH112 module
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaERF1 is induced by cold/JA in A. annua. 2. It directly binds and activates ADS and CYP71AV1 promoters. 3. This enhances artemisinin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value regulator for artemisinin engineering, especially in combination with upstream cold/JA-responsive TFs.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of artemisinin biosynthesis under cold-associated conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator of key artemisinin biosynthetic promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding assays summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaERF2 is JA responsive and induced under cold-associated artemisinin accumulation conditions. 2. It binds promoters of ADS and CYP71AV1. 3. Its action contributes to higher artemisinin production.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for artemisinin pathway activation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: synergistically activates DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes artemisinin accumulation by activating indispensable late biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct positive controller of a productive branch toward artemisinin.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activation evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaORA is a JA-responsive, trichome-specific AP2/ERF TF. 2. Together with AaTCP14 it activates DBR2 and ALDH1. 3. This supports increased artemisinin biosynthesis under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent regulator for targeted enhancement of artemisinin biosynthesis in glandular trichomes.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: synergistically activates DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances artemisinin biosynthesis by co-activating late pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supports decisive activation of indispensable late biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activation evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaTCP14 cooperates with AaORA. 2. The module activates DBR2 and ALDH1 promoters. 3. This promotes artemisinin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate co-regulator for stacked TF engineering of artemisinin.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates ALDH1 expression by direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Prominently promotes artemisinin biosynthesis and accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highlighted in review as playing a prominent role in artemisinin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Direct promoter binding and expression activation summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cold elevates ABA-related signaling in A. annua. 2. AaABF3 directly binds the ALDH1 promoter. 3. This activates artemisinin biosynthesis and accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong regulator for ABA-linked artemisinin improvement strategies.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates AaERF1 promoter and enhances artemisinin-pathway promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes artemisinin production under cold-responsive regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as an upstream positive regulator linking cold to ERF-mediated pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Dual-luciferase assay; Y1H; overexpression summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AabHLH112 binds the AaERF1 promoter. 2. It enhances artemisinin biosynthesis gene promoter activity. 3. Overexpression increases AaERF1 expression and artemisinin production.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority upstream regulator for cold-inducible artemisinin engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positive regulation of artemisinin biosynthesis genes implied
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced AaMYC2-Like increases artemisinin contents by about 4-fold.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important integrator of cold and hormone signals for artemisinin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis and metabolite profiling summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaMYC2-Like is strongly responsive to temperature stress. 2. Cold increases its transcription and artemisinin content by about 4-fold. 3. Hormone induction suggests combined cold–phytohormone control.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising integrative regulator for inducible artemisinin improvement.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Brassica oleracea var. acephala f. tricolor
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates a subset of anthocyanin biosynthesis genes including DFR and ANS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly induced by cold and drives an extreme increase in anthocyanin accumulation, up to about 50-fold in purple kale.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: The review highlights BoPAP1 as one of the most effective cold-induced TFs for anthocyanin synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR analysis and metabolite profiling summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BoPAP1 is strongly induced by cold in purple kale. 2. It activates DFR and ANS expression. 3. Anthocyanin content rises about 50-fold, the most dramatic example emphasized in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Exceptional candidate for metabolic engineering or breeding of high-anthocyanin crops.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive MYB associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Listed by review as a cold-responsive anthocyanin regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from cited cold-induction study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LIMYB3 is induced by cold. 2. It is associated with anthocyanin accumulation in lily. 3. It represents a cold-responsive MYB regulator of flavonoid biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential ornamental and nutritional pigmentation regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MBW complex with bHLH TF and WD-repeat protein
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Cold-induced demethylation of the CsAN1 promoter increases expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-induced promoter demethylation upregulates CsAN1 and promotes anthocyanin accumulation in tea leaves.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Epigenetically activated MYB regulator within the MBW system for anthocyanin synthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter methylation analysis and gene expression summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsAN1 is a MYB regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in tea. 2. Cold induces demethylation of its promoter. 3. Increased CsAN1 expression promotes anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for epigenetic breeding and cool-induced quality improvement in tea.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: enhances MdbHLH33 binding to MdCBF2 and MdDFR promoters
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cold stress; inhibited ubiquitination via MdMIEL1 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases anthocyanin accumulation in apple under cold conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cold-sensitive MYB that reinforces bHLH-mediated activation of anthocyanin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction and overexpression evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB308L interacts with MdbHLH33. 2. It enhances MdbHLH33 association with MdCBF2 and MdDFR promoters. 3. Overexpression elevates anthocyanin accumulation under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for improving apple coloration and cold response.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes ANS and UFGT promoter activity under cold
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive MYB that promotes anthocyanin accumulation in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Directly associated with enhanced activity of late anthocyanin biosynthetic promoters under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter activity evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYBPA1 is induced by cool conditions. 2. Cold enhances its effect on ANS and UFGT promoter activities. 3. It positively regulates anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for color enhancement in apple breeding.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdANR directly and associates with MdCBFs for cold tolerance-related regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cold stress; stabilization through repression of MdBT2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proanthocyanidin/anthocyanin-related accumulation and cold tolerance in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cold-stabilized MYB regulator integrating flavonoid synthesis and cold response.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter interaction and protein stability evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB23 directly targets MdANR under cold. 2. It also associates with MdCBFs to improve cold tolerance. 3. Cold suppresses MdBT2, stabilizing MdMYB23 and favoring flavonoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable target for simultaneous improvement of cold tolerance and flavonoid traits.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdCCA1 by binding AACCG motif in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: May act through CBF-dependent pathway via MdCCA1 to MdCBF
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation, although the direct downstream anthocyanin mechanism remains unclear.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cold-induced MYB linked to CBF-dependent anthocyanin regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdMYB88/124 are induced by cold. 2. They directly activate MdCCA1, which controls MdCBF expression. 3. They are proposed to promote anthocyanins through a CBF-dependent pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct anthocyanin biosynthetic targets were not clearly established in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential integrator of circadian/cold signaling for coloration traits.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates MdDFR and MdUFGT directly; activates MdBBX20; enhances MdMYB1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Participates in MBW regulatory system with MYB partners
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A central cold-responsive activator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Directly binds multiple anthocyanin gene promoters and coordinates MBW-related activation under cool conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; EMSA; phosphorylation evidence; overexpression summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbHLH3 interacts with MdMYB1 and functions in the MBW system. 2. It directly binds MdDFR and MdUFGT promoters and also activates MdBBX20. 3. Cool-induced phosphorylation increases its promoter-binding activity and anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major regulator for improving apple skin coloration under cool climates.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: positively regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis genes through MYB interactions
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with multiple MYB TFs in the MBW regulatory system
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of both cold tolerance and anthocyanin biosynthesis in apple.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highlighted as a central bHLH in the MBW system during cold-induced anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdbHLH33 is a positive regulator of cold tolerance and anthocyanin biosynthesis. 2. It interacts with several MYB TFs, notably MdMYB308L. 3. These interactions enhance downstream anthocyanin gene expression under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for coordinated improvement of cold tolerance and pigmentation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates transcription of anthocyanin biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by low temperature and increases anthocyanin content by about 5-fold in tomato seedlings.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the clearest cold-induced bHLH regulators for anthocyanin accumulation discussed in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; cold treatment; transcript and metabolite analyses summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AH was identified by map-based cloning in tomato. 2. Low temperature induces AH expression. 3. AH upregulates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes and raises anthocyanin levels about 5-fold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding cool-responsive pigmented tomato lines.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin biosynthesis through MYB-bHLH complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold significantly induces NtAN2, enabling strong anthocyanin accumulation in Lc-transgenic tobacco lines.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as the MYB partner in a cold-induced Lc-NtAN2 activating complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cold induction and transgenic complex-function evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NtAN2 is a cold-induced R2R3-MYB. 2. It forms a complex with Lc. 3. This complex activates anthocyanin biosynthesis and greatly increases pigmentation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good candidate for combinatorial engineering with bHLH partners.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms Lc-NtAN2 complex in transgenic tobacco
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates anthocyanin biosynthesis as MYB-bHLH complex partner
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cold stress indirectly through NtAN2 induction in Lc-transgenic lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lc cooperates with cold-induced NtAN2 to promote anthocyanin biosynthesis in transgenic tobacco.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides the bHLH component of the cold-activated MYB-bHLH module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic functional evidence summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lc is a classic anthocyanin-promoting bHLH. 2. In Lc-transgenic tobacco, cold-induced NtAN2 partners with Lc. 3. The complex drives anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Widely useful bHLH for anthocyanin engineering across species.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive MYB associated with about 3-fold increase in flavonoid-related output.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Listed as a representative cold-responsive MYB regulator of flavonoids.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from cited study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PbrMYB5 is induced at 4°C. 2. It is linked to increased flavonoid accumulation. 3. It represents pear MYB regulation under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for improving flavonoid quality traits in pear rootstock or fruit systems.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Listed in the review as a cold-responsive MYB involved in flavonoid regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative cold-induced MYB summarized for flavonoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from cited study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmMYB31 is included among cold-responsive MYB TFs. 2. It is associated with flavonoid metabolic regulation. 3. Detailed direct targets are not provided in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic detail in this review is limited.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold induces PgMYB1 transcript strongly, but its direct effect on secondary metabolites remains unclear.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential regulator summarized by the review, needing further functional exploration.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PgMYB1 transcript increases about 3.9-fold after 4°C treatment. 2. It is also hormone inducible. 3. Its role in ginseng secondary metabolism is still unresolved.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review explicitly states that its effect on secondary metabolites still needs further exploration.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: upregulates GoLS and raffinose synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of raffinose family oligosaccharides under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cold-induced regulator of protective sugar metabolism summarized in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression association summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AQUILO is cold induced in grape. 2. It upregulates GoLS and raffinose synthase genes. 3. This promotes raffinose family oligosaccharide accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for cold tolerance and carbohydrate quality engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold induces AaNAC1 and it is proposed as a key regulator in stress signaling related to artemisinin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate regulator requiring continued study.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AaNAC1 is induced by cold in A. annua. 2. It may function as a key stress signaling regulator. 3. The review links it to cold-responsive artemisinin regulation but with limited mechanistic detail.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct targets were not specified in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator to increase carotenoid accumulation under cold stress.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative NAC regulator linking cold to terpenoid accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Summarized from cited study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlNAC4 can be induced by cold. 2. It positively regulates carotenoid accumulation. 3. It exemplifies NAC involvement in cold-responsive terpenoid metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving carotenoid accumulation and chilling response in tomato.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: expression profile parallels multiple MVA/MEP pathway genes affecting metabolite accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cold-responsive WRKY associated with changes in blinin and total saponin content.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highlighted regulator candidate coordinating MVA and MEP pathway gene responses under cold.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling summarized in review
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CbWRKY24 peaks at 6 h under 4°C. 2. Its pattern parallels several MVA/MEP pathway genes. 3. These changes correlate with blinin and total saponin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct DNA binding to the listed genes was not established in this review.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for diterpenoid/saponin metabolic engineering in medicinal plants.
